Transcrição Completa: Why Full Time Closers Are Better

[00:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "16 Why Full Time Closers Are Better"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Título com comparação entre equipes internas vs terceirizadas

[00:02] In business, when you look at sales teams, there's currently a lot of business owners that are very laxidasical in how they go about running their sales organizations. As you've seen throughout these last couple videos, and I want to be very clear when I say this, there's currently two options.

[00:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Two Options for Business Owners"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Duas opções: In-house sales team vs Outsourced closing team

[00:22] And those two options are very simple. You're either going to build an in-house sales organization, so an in-house sales team, or you're going to use an outsourced closing team.

[00:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Sales = Time & Effort Intensive Job"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Vendas como trabalho que exige dedicação full-time

[00:35] Now, when it comes to sales people, it's a very time and effort intensive job. What I mean by that is we can't have people who are part-time. We need people who are full-time in the organization to get the most results.

[00:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "In-House Teams: More Responsibility"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Responsabilidades: hiring, training, developing ongoing

[00:52] In-house sales teams are harder, they take more time. They're a little harder to develop because you as the business owner are going to be responsible for hiring great people, training those people on an ongoing basis, and developing those people on an ongoing basis.

[01:08] That requires a level of responsibility that some business owners just simply put, they don't want to do, and they push it off onto what's called an outsourced sales team.

[01:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Outsourced Teams: Hands-Off Appeal"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Promessa de outsourcing: hire, train, staff for you

[01:22] Outsourced sales teams, they typically have all of those responsibilities handled themselves. They'll hire people for you. They'll train people for you. They'll staff people onto your account. And essentially the sell in that type of system is that the business owner can be a little more hands-off.

[01:40] Now, it sounds great, but in practice, it is the most limiting possible thing that you could do to your business.

[01:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Jeremy's Experience: Long-term vs Short-term"

NÚMEROS: 7-8 year deals, millions per month vs plateau/decline

VISUAL: Comparação de resultados: in-house sustentável vs outsourced limitado

[01:52] From my experience, and to be fair, I have had a few outsourced sales teams that we've worked with that have done quite well through the years, but they've all been short-term deals. I have deals that have lasted literally seven to eight years, multi-year relationships that make millions of dollars per month.

[02:10] I have some businesses that we've done with outsourced closing teams that have also gotten to a million dollars a month, but then things start to plateau or things start to decline.

[02:20] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Incentive Alignment Problem"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Outsourced incentives vs company incentives não alinhados

[02:25] And it's never a shock when that occurs because outsourced closing teams don't have the strongest incentive to continue growing your business. Their incentives are their own incentives. In-house closing teams are the company's incentives.

[02:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Real Example: Client Gets Fired"

NÚMEROS: $20K upfront + 20% gross, 6-10 deals/day vs every other day

VISUAL: Cliente sendo demitido por equipe terceirizada por baixo volume

[02:42] What do I mean by that? Well, I just recently referred a client to an outsourced closing team. That outsourced sales team came in. They charged 20K right off the bat just to start. After that, they took 20% gross of each sale.

[02:58] However, in the first two weeks of that deal, that sales team did quite well. They were closing a deal every other day. Then after two weeks, they fired the client and they refunded the original 20k.

[03:12] And the closer calls me. He goes, "Hey, I'm sorry I had to fire that client. We have another deal that we're doing that has six deals a day being closed. And simply put, I can't continue to put the time and attention into this client because they're only closing a deal every other day."

[03:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Outsourced Team Problems"

NÚMEROS: 8-10 call slots split between multiple companies

VISUAL: Problemas: part-time, fat cat closers, only scheduled calls

[03:32] "So, I have to take my closers off that account and I have to put them onto this other account that we're doing six to 10 deals a day on." That's very fair, but obviously that puts the client in a tough spot.

[03:45] Now, the other kind of negative incentives of an outsource sales team is they're not going to be full-time for you naturally, right off the bat. They're going to be part-time.

[03:55] Outsource sales teams typically also are filled with fat cat closers. They will not follow up on your organization's behalf with old leads. They will not dial to people who have filled out lead forms.

[04:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Call Slot Distribution Problem"

NÚMEROS: 3-4 slots company A, 2-3 slots company B, 1-2 slots for you

VISUAL: Divisão de tempo entre múltiplos clientes

[04:12] They absolutely hate those strategies and they are terrible with them because they're fat cat closers. Most of these outsource sales teams will only take scheduled calls. Out of their entire day, let's say they have eight to 10 call slots a day. Maybe three or four of them are filled up with a different company, two or three with another, and then maybe one or two slots available for you.

[04:32] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "In-House Teams Excel Where Outsourced Fail"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: In-house fazendo tudo que outsourced evita: forms, outbound, follow-up

[04:35] In-house sales teams, on the other hand, do extremely well with everything that the outsource sales teams don't do well. They will dial on inbound leads that filled out forms. They will do cold pitching, outbound dialing. They will take scheduled calls. They will follow up with old leads.

[04:52] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Full Focus on Your Business"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Vendedor dedicado 100% ao seu produto vs dividido entre vários

[04:55] They will spend their full entire day and their entire section of their career where they're working with you developing their skills around selling your offers, your products, and your solutions. Their entire focus goes towards being better at selling whatever it is that your business sells.

[05:12] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Jeremy's Strong Recommendation"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Recomendação clara: bias towards in-house teams

[05:15] I heavily encourage every client that I have to bias towards in-house teams. I am not a fan of the outsource sales teams just based on an incentive perspective.

[05:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO

─────────────────────────────

TEXTO: "Your Responsibility as Business Owner"

NÚMEROS: None

VISUAL: Lista de responsabilidades: hiring, training, developing, tracking, making great

[05:28] So, when you're looking for full-time closers, you're going to have to take on the responsibility of hiring, training, developing your sales teams, tracking them statistically, and making them great.

[05:38] The appeal in the offer of what outsourced sales teams I agree is appealing but in practice very limiting.

🔥 ANÁLISE FURION POWER: Jeremy ANIQUILA completamente a ilusão das equipes terceirizadas! O exemplo brutal do cliente sendo DEMITIDO pela própria equipe de vendas expõe a realidade cruel: você não é prioridade, é apenas uma fonte de receita secundária. Jeremy destrói a mentalidade preguiçosa de donos de negócio que querem terceirizar o que mais importa. In-house teams = LEALDADE TOTAL. Outsourced = fat cat closers divididos. Jeremy força você a ASSUMIR responsabilidade pelo que faz sua empresa BILHÕES!
